mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns following UNRATE jumps > 0.5pp are not statistically negative (mean return > 0, p > 0.05) over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation readings and aggressive federal funds rate hikes compress real household disposable income and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are in their top quartile, or interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indexes indicate successful supply chain normalization and pent-up demand fulfillment, boosting cyclical equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly scales forward corporate earnings expectations, producing a linear positive relationship between macroeconomic expansion and S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward equity returns, or rolling correlation falls below 0.1 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.4 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in the construction sector and durable goods demand, dragging down equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns following HOUST drops below 1400 are not significantly negative (mean > 0, p > 0.05) over the 20-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a soft-landing labor market, supporting equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability does not significantly predict positive 20-day returns, or out-of-sample IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: During a high-inflation monetary tightening regime, elevated policy rates compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds its 80th percentile threshold, or volatility-adjusted returns show no decline.
